title: A practical Laser-Heated Diamond Anvil Cell synthesis technique and recovery workflow for metastable MnSb2 and YbZn2 phases
authors: S. Huyan, R. F. S. Penacchio, D. Zhang, Z. Li, S. L. Morelhão, Raquel Ribeiro, P. C. Canfield, S. L. Bud'ko
abstract: The creation and exploration of new materials under extreme pressure-temperature conditions has become increasingly reliant on laser-heated diamond anvil cell (LHDAC) techniques, which provide direct access to previously unexplored regions of multinary phase diagrams. Whereas numerous high-pressure phases have been identified in situ, systematic recovery and post-synthesis physical property characterization of these materials remain significant challenges. In this work, we present the development of an integrated LHDAC synthesis and demonstrate a practical LHDAC-based synthesis workflow that enables stabilization and recovery of metastable intermetallic phases for subsequent structural and transport studies. Using this approach, we successfully achieved LHDAC synthesis of high-pressure MnSb2 and YbZn2 phases under moderate pressures. Synchrotron X-ray diffraction and spatial mapping confirm dominant formation of the targeted phases, whereas laboratory-based refinement quantifies phase fractions despite intrinsic microstrain and minor secondary phases. High-pressure transport measurements on recovered samples reveal tunable by pressure electronic instabilities in both systems. In MnSb2, pressure suppresses two high-temperature magnetic ordering anomalies, observed in transport, by 5 GPa and for higher pressures induces a new low-temperature feature that increases with further pressure increase. In hexagonal high-pressure YbZn2, an electronic reconstruction emerges at ~11 GPa, characterized by semiconducting-like behavior from ~ 30 K to 300 K and a broad low-temperature coherence crossover near 30 K. Our results establish LHDAC synthesis not only as a structural discovery tool, but also as an experimental platform for investigating correlated quantum states stabilized far from equilibrium thermodynamic conditions.
summary: This work establishes a practical, integrated workflow for synthesizing and characterizing metastable high-pressure phases using Laser-Heated Diamond Anvil Cells. By studying $	ext{MnSb}_2$ and $	ext{YbZn}_2$, the authors demonstrate that these materials exhibit highly tunable electronic and magnetic instabilities governed by pressure. This advances LHDAC from a mere structural discovery tool to a powerful platform for investigating correlated quantum states.
main_problem: The primary challenge addressed is the systematic recovery and post-synthesis physical characterization of metastable intermetallic phases created under extreme, non-equilibrium pressure-temperature conditions using Laser-Heated Diamond Anvil Cells (LHDAC).
main_result: The authors successfully demonstrated an integrated LHDAC workflow, revealing tunable electronic instabilities in both $	ext{MnSb}_2$ and $	ext{YbZn}_2$, which exhibit pressure-induced changes in magnetic and electronic ground states.
method: The study combines LHDAC synthesis with structural characterization via synchrotron X-ray diffraction and detailed transport measurements ($R$ vs. $T$ and $P$) on recovered samples.
model_or_system: The research focuses on metastable intermetallic compounds, specifically $	ext{MnSb}_2$ and $	ext{YbZn}_2$, whose electronic and magnetic properties are investigated when stabilized far from equilibrium thermodynamic conditions.
key_observables: Electrical resistance ($ho$), temperature dependence of resistance ($	ext{d}R/	ext{d}T$), magnetic ordering anomalies, and phase fractions derived from XRD.
important_parameters: High pressures (up to $\sim 15 	ext{ GPa}$), high temperatures (up to $\sim 3000 	ext{ K}$), and specific pressure thresholds (e.g., $\sim 11 	ext{ GPa}$ for $	ext{YbZn}_2$).
main_assumptions: The primary limitation acknowledged is the inherent spatial inhomogeneity due to localized laser heating, necessitating careful selection of high-purity sample regions for reliable measurement.
figures_summary: Figures illustrate the pressure dependence of electrical resistance for $	ext{YbZn}_2$, showing an abrupt transition to semiconducting-like behavior near $11 	ext{ GPa}$, and show the pressure suppression of magnetic anomalies in $	ext{MnSb}_2$.
paper_structure: The paper follows a structure of problem identification (LHDAC recovery challenge), methodology development (integrated synthesis/recovery workflow), structural confirmation (XRD mapping), and detailed physical characterization (transport measurements on recovered samples).
why_interesting: The ability to probe correlated quantum states (like magnetic or electronic instabilities) in materials synthesized under extreme, non-equilibrium conditions provides a unique experimental playground for testing theories of strongly correlated electron systems.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
